criteria.evidence: Every story has S/T/A/R/L; e.g. Story 1 has S (pricing A/B test), T (confound mid-test), A (three steps: segment cohort / re-run clean / dual-report), R (4.1->5.0% held), L (name the confound out loud).
criteria.reason: All five STAR elements present in all 5 stories, with three distinct, decision-flavored actions each.

criteria.evidence: 'I identified and segmented out the campaign-exposed cohort... I re-ran... I reported both reads' (Story 1); 'I made the case for an A/B test' (Story 2). Story 2 R uses 'we didn't ship it'.
criteria.reason: Individual ownership is consistently clear via 'I' for the decisions; a couple of collective 'we' on outcomes (we didn't ship) is contextually accurate and minor.

criteria.evidence: 4.1%->5.0% (persona l.19), -8% retention (persona l.44), AUC ~0.78 (persona l.21), ~6 analyst-hrs/wk (persona l.25). Story 4 R: 'needs a number - outcome is in progress'.
criteria.reason: Every number traces to the persona; zero fabrication. The production-ML story correctly flags its result rather than inventing a deployment metric. Exactly the anti-trap behavior required.

criteria.evidence: L lines are generalizable, e.g. 'A primary metric without a guardrail can hide the real damage' (Story 2); 'Owning a real gap with a concrete plan reads as judgment' (Story 4).
criteria.reason: Each story has one tight, transferable lesson.

criteria.evidence: JD->story map maps each JD line to a primary + backup story with reuse (Story 1/2 reused as backups); 'practice each aloud, timed to ~90s'. Some bullets (Story 1 A) are dense for a 90s spoken delivery.
criteria.reason: 5 stories with a deliberate reuse map covering the role's question types; slight length risk but the 'Strengthen before the room' notes flag tightening.

criteria.evidence: Story 1 = judgment-under-ambiguity (confound); Story 2 = disagreeing-up/influence-with-data (paywall retention catch); Story 3+4 = modeling told honestly, Story 4 explicitly names the production-ML gap and does NOT claim deployment.
criteria.reason: Covers exactly the expected archetypes; the modeling story is split into offline-craft (S3) and honest-gap (S4), neither inflating production ML.

highest_leverage_fix: None required - passing. Optional: have build-stories trim multi-clause Action bullets so each story is verifiably <=90s spoken, not just labeled so.
